functions.label: applyBeforeToolCallResult
signature: (context: ToolCallHookContextBase, result: void | { toolParams?: Record<string, unknown> | undefined; }) => ToolCallHookContextBase

functions.label: chatSystemIndex
description: Helper function to define chat system indices.

functions.label: describeZodSchema
description:
Extracts a human-readable list of field descriptions from a Zod object schema
using the public `z.toJSONSchema()` API.

Used to describe connector sub-action parameters to the agent LLM.

functions.label: formatSchemaForLlm
description:
Formats a Zod schema into a multi-line parameter summary string for LLM consumption.

Example output:
  "- query (string, required): Search query to find messages\n- inChannel (string, optional): Channel name"

functions.label: getAgentFromRunContext
description: Returns the most recent agent entry from a run context stack, if any.

functions.label: getToolResultId
description: Generate a random id which can be used for tool result id.

functions.label: isToolResultId
description: Check if the provided string is a valid tool result id.

interfaces.label: AgentBuilderPluginSetup
description: Setup contract of the agentBuilder plugin.

children.label: agents
description: Agents setup contract, which can be used to register built-in agents.

children.label: tools
description: Tools setup contract, which can be used to register built-in tools.

children.label: attachments
description: Attachments setup contract, which can be used to register attachment types.

children.label: hooks
description: Hooks setup contract, which can be used to register lifecycle event hooks.

children.label: skills
description: Skills setup contract, which can be used to register skills.

children.label: plugins
description: Plugins setup contract, which can be used to register built-in plugins.

children.label: topSnippets
description:
TOP_SNIPPETS configuration (numSnippets, numWords) from `xpack.agentBuilder.topSnippets`.
Exposed so that dependent plugins can pass these values to search utilities.

interfaces.label: AgentBuilderPluginStart
description: Start contract of the agentBuilder plugin.

children.label: agents
description: Agents service, to execute agents.

children.label: tools
description: Tools service, to manage or execute tools.

children.label: skills
description: Skills service, to manage and access skills.

children.label: plugins
description: Plugins service, to query built-in and persisted plugins.

children.label: execution
description: Execution service, to execute agents and retrieve execution status.

children.label: runtime
description:
Runtime utilities for consumers that need to interact with LLM models
outside of the agent builder's built-in tool/agent execution flow.

children.label: conversations
description: Conversations service (read-only), to list and retrieve conversations.

interfaces.label: AgentExecutionService
description:
The agent execution service - entry point for deferred agent execution.
Replaces the direct call to ChatService.converse in the request flow.

children.label: executeAgent
description:
Execute an agent, either locally or on a Task Manager node.
Creates an execution document and returns the execution ID along with an events observable.

children.label: getExecution
description: Retrieve an agent execution by its ID.

children.label: abortExecution
description:
Abort an ongoing execution.
Sets the execution status to 'aborted', which the TM handler will detect via polling.

children.label: followExecution
description:
Follow an execution by polling for events.
Returns an observable that emits events as they are written to the data stream.
The observable completes when the execution reaches a terminal status.

children.label: findExecutions
description:
Find executions matching the given filters. Defaults to current space unless spaceId is explicitly provided.
Callers that override spaceId are responsible for their own authorization.
Note: returned executions have `events: []` (events are excluded for performance). Use `getExecution` or `readEvents` for full events.

children.label: request
description:
The request that was provided when initiating that tool execution.
Can be used to create scoped services not directly exposed by this context.

children.label: spaceId
description: Id of the space associated with the request

children.label: defaultConnectorId
description: The resolved connector ID for this execution, if any.

children.label: esClient
description:
A cluster client scoped to the current user.
Can be used to access ES on behalf of either the current user or the system user.

children.label: savedObjectsClient
description: Saved objects client scoped to the current user.

children.label: modelProvider
description:
Inference model provider scoped to the current user.
Can be used to access the inference APIs or chatModel.

children.label: toolProvider
description: Tool provider that can be used to list or execute tools.

children.label: toolRegistry
description:
Tool registry for accessing internal tool definitions.
Used for features like tool-specific result summarization.

children.label: runner
description: AgentBuilder runner scoped to the current execution.

children.label: attachments
description: Attachment service to interact with attachments.

children.label: skills
description: Skills service to interact with skills.

children.label: plugins
description: Plugins service to resolve plugin-contributed skill IDs during execution.

children.label: toolManager
description: Tool manager to manage active tools for the agent.

children.label: resultStore
description: Result store to access and add tool results during execution.

children.label: skillsStore
description:
Skills store to populate with filtered skills during execution.
Backs the skills volume in the virtual filesystem.

children.label: attachmentStateManager
description: Attachment state manager to manage conversation attachments during execution.

children.label: promptManager
description: Used to manage interruptions.

children.label: stateManager
description: Used to access and store state during interrupted executions.

children.label: events
description: Event emitter that can be used to emits custom events

children.label: logger
description: Logger scoped to this execution

children.label: hooks
description: Hooks service for agent lifecycle interception.

children.label: filestore
description: File store to access data from the agent's virtual filesystem

children.label: experimentalFeatures
description:
Experimental features configuration for this agent execution.
Determined by the UI setting at the start of execution.

children.label: executionMode
description:
The execution mode for this agent run.
NOTE: atm, when 'standalone', the execution is non-interactive (HITL disabled).

children.label: subAgentExecutor
description: Sub-agent executor for spawning child agent executions.

children.label: agentParams
description: The params that the agent execution API was called with

children.label: runId
description: ID of this run

children.label: abortSignal
description: optional signal to abort the execution of the agent

children.label: result
description: The plain result of the agent

children.label: register
description: Register a built-in agent to be available in agentBuilder.

children.label: runAgent
description: Executes an agent with the given parameters.

children.label: getRegistry
description: Return an agent registry scoped to the current user and context.

children.label: registerType
description: Register an attachment type to be available in agentBuilder.

interfaces.label: BuiltinToolDefinition
description: Built-in tool, as registered as static tool.

children.label: type
description: built-in tool types

children.label: schema
description: Tool's input schema, defined as a zod schema.

children.label: handler
description: Handler to call to execute the tool.

children.label: availability
description:
Optional dynamic availability configuration.
Refer to

interfaces.label: ConversationsStart
description: AgentBuilder conversations service's start contract (read-only).

children.label: getScopedClient
description: Returns a read-only conversation client scoped to the given request's user and space.

interfaces.label: ExecutableTool
description: AgentBuilder tool, as exposed by tool providers.

children.label: getSchema
description: Tool's input schema, defined as a zod schema.

children.label: execute
description: Run handler that can be used to execute the tool.

children.label: params
signature: | undefined; toolParams: TParams; toolCallId?: string | undefined; defaultConnectorId?: string | undefined; }

children.label: getLlmDescription
description:
Optional handled to add additional instructions to the LLM.
When provided, will replace the description when converting to llm tool.

children.label: summarizeToolReturn
description:
Optional function to summarize a tool return for conversation history.
When provided, this function will be called when processing conversation history
to replace large tool results with compact summaries.

interfaces.label: ExecuteAgentResult
description: Result of executing an agent.

children.label: executionId
description: The unique execution ID.

children.label: events$
description:
Observable of events for this execution.
- Local mode: the live agent event stream (multicasted).
- TM mode: polls the data stream (equivalent to followExecution).

interfaces.label: ExecutionStart
description: AgentBuilder execution service's start contract.

children.label: executeAgent
description: Execute an agent.

children.label: getExecution
description: Retrieve an agent execution by its ID.

children.label: findExecutions
description: Find executions matching the given filters.

interfaces.label: ExperimentalFeatures
description: Experimental features configuration for agent builder.

children.label: filestore
description: Whether the filestore feature is enabled

children.label: skills
description: Whether the skills feature is enabled

children.label: subagents
description: Whether the sub-agent execution feature is enabled

interfaces.label: FindExecutionsFilter
description: Filter criteria for

.
All specified fields are ANDed together.

children.label: metadata
description: Match executions whose metadata contains all specified key-value pairs.

children.label: status
description: Match executions with one of these statuses.

interfaces.label: FindExecutionsOptions
description: Options for

children.label: spaceId
description: Space to scope results to. Defaults to current space from request if omitted.

children.label: filter
description: Filter criteria. All specified fields are ANDed together.

children.label: size
description: Maximum number of results to return. Defaults to 10.

children.label: sort
description:
Sort field and order. Defaults to `{ field: '@timestamp', order: 'desc' }`.
Note: field is an explicit union rather than derived from AgentExecutionProperties to avoid
circular dependencies and to prevent exposing snake_case ES field names to consumers.

interfaces.label: HookHandlerResultByLifecycle
description: Define which return type each hook lifecycle supports.

children.label: register
description: Register one or more lifecycle hooks in a single call.

children.label: run
description:
Runs blocking hooks first (await), then runs non-blocking hooks with the updated context (fire-and-forget).
Returns the context as updated by blocking hooks.

interfaces.label: InternalToolDefinition
description: Internal generic representation for a tool definition

children.label: experimental
description: When true, this tool is only available when experimental features are enabled.

children.label: isAvailable
description: Check if the tool is available for the current context.

children.label: getSchema
description: Generates the schema attached to this tool.

children.label: getHandler
description: Get the handler which can be used to execute the tool.

children.label: getLlmDescription
description:
Optional handled to add additional instructions to the LLM
when specified, this will fully replace the description when converting to LLM tools.

children.label: summarizeToolReturn
description:
Optional function to summarize a tool return for conversation history.
When provided, this function will be called when processing conversation history
to replace large tool results with compact summaries.

children.label: confirmation
description: Tool call policy to control tool call confirmation behavior

interfaces.label: ModelProvider
description: Provider, allowing to select various models depending on the needs.

children.label: getDefaultModel
description: Returns the default model used for LLM tasks in the current context.

children.label: selectModel
description:
Returns the best available model based on the given selection options.

Available options are:
- effortLevel: the effort level of the task the model will be used for.

children.label: getModelById
description:
Returns a model using the given connectorId.

Will throw if connector doesn't exist, user has no access, or connector is not a GenAI connector.

children.label: getUsageStats
description: Returns the current usage stats for the model provider.

children.label: register
description:
Register a built-in plugin to be available in agentBuilder.
Built-in plugins are read-only and registered programmatically by solution teams.

interfaces.label: PluginsStart
description: AgentBuilder plugins service's start contract.

children.label: getRegistry
description:
Return a plugin registry scoped to the current user and context.
The registry provides access to both built-in and persisted plugins.

interfaces.label: ProcessedAttachment
description: Server-side processed attachment: attachment plus its representation and tools.

interfaces.label: ProcessedRoundInput
description: Processed input for a single conversation round (message + processed attachments).

interfaces.label: ReadOnlyConversationClient
description: A read-only conversation client exposing only get and list operations.

children.label: get
description: Retrieve a single conversation by its ID, including all rounds.

children.label: list
description: List conversations for the current user, optionally filtered by agent ID.

description: Params for

description: Execution mode for this run. When 'standalone', HITL is disabled.

children.label: agentId
description: ID of the agent to call.

children.label: agentParams
description: Parameters to call the agent with.

children.label: executionId
description: ID of the agent execution to associate with the run.

children.label: onEvent
description: Optional event handler.

children.label: abortSignal
description: Optional signal to abort the execution of the agent.

children.label: request
description: The request that initiated that run.

children.label: defaultConnectorId
description:
Optional genAI connector id to use as default.
If unspecified, will use internal logic to use the default connector

children.label: result
description: return from the agent

interfaces.label: RunContext
description:
Context bound to a run execution.
Contains metadata associated with the run's current state.
Will be attached to errors thrown during a run.
It is serializable.

children.label: runId
description: The run identifier, which can be used for tracing

children.label: stack
description: The current execution stack

interfaces.label: Runner
description:
Represents a runner, which is the entry point to execute all agentBuilder primitives,
such as tools or agents.

This version is not scoped to a given request, and is the version exposed from the plugin's contract.

description: Execute a tool (based on its ID).

description: Execute an internal tool definition.

description: AgentBuilder runtime service's start contract.

Creates a model provider for the given request context.
The model provider can be used to obtain a ScopedModel for use
with utilities like `generateEsql` from `@kbn/agent-builder-genai-utils`.
signature: <unknown, unknown, unknown, any>; defaultConnectorId?: string | undefined; }) =>

interfaces.label: RunToolParams
description: Params for

children.label: toolId
description: ID of the tool to call.

children.label: toolParams
description: Parameters to call the tool with.

children.label: toolCallId
description: Optional toolCall id associated with the tool invocation.

children.label: source
description:
Optional source of the tool invocation.
Defaults to 'unknown'.

description: Optional prompt storage state to use for tool invocation.

description: Optional event handler.

description: The request that initiated that run.

description:
Optional genAI connector id to use as default.
If unspecified, will use internal logic to use the default connector
(EIS if there, otherwise openAI, otherwise any GenAI)

description:
Optional abort signal for the run (e.g. from the request).
Propagated to hooks so they can respect cancellation.

description:
Return type for tool invocation APIs.

Wrapping the plain result to allow extending the shape later without
introducing breaking changes.

children.label: prompt
description: Interruption returned by the tool

interfaces.label: ScopedModel

Represents a model that can be used within the agentBuilder framework (e.g. tools).

It exposes different interfaces to models.

description: The connector bound to this scoped model.

children.label: chatModel
description: langchain chat model.

children.label: inferenceClient
description: Inference client.

interfaces.label: ScopedRunner
description:
Represents a runner, which is the entry point to execute all agentBuilder primitives,
such as tools or agents.

This version is pre-scoped to a given request, meaning APIs don't need to be passed
down a request object.

description: AgentBuilder skills service's start contract.

children.label: getRegistry
description:
Create a skill registry scoped to the current user and context.
The registry provides access to both built-in and persisted skills.

children.label: register
description:
Register a skill dynamically after plugin start.
Only affects future conversations (existing ones snapshot skills at creation time).

interfaces.label: SubAgentExecutor
description:
Pre-scoped executor for spawning sub-agent executions.
The `request` is already bound — callers don't need to provide it.

children.label: executeSubAgent
description: Execute a sub-agent and return the execution ID and events observable.

| undefined; parentExecutionId: string; prompt: string; abortSignal?: AbortSignal | undefined; }) => Promise<{ executionId: string; events$:

children.label: getExecution
description: Retrieve a sub-agent execution by ID. Returns undefined if not found.

children.label: handler
description:
handler which can be defined to add conditional availability of the tool.

Note: this is meant to be used for tools that are gated behind a feature flag,
      or tools which have some condition to be available.
      it *IS NOT* meant to be used as a replacement for RBAC.

children.label: cacheMode
description:
Cache mode for the result
- global: the result will be cached globally, for all spaces
- space: the result will be cached per-space
- none: the result shouldn't be cached (warning: this can lead to performance issues)

children.label: cacheTtl
description:
Optional TTL for the cached result, *in seconds*.
Default to 300 seconds (5 minutes).

description: Information exposed to the

Emit a UI event which can be listened to on the front-end.

Scoped context which can be used during tool execution to access

Inference model provider scoped to the current user.

Registry for managing tools within an agent execution context.

interfaces.label: ToolStateManager
description:
Tool state manager - Which can be used to persist state between executions of the tool.

Note: state is bound to a tool call, and not shared between multiple calls to the same tool.

children.label: getState
description:
Retrieve the state which was stored during the previous execution of the tool.
This is only useful when interrupting the tool to prompt the user,
to retrieve the state when the execution is resumed.

children.label: setState
description:
Persist the internal state of the tool execution, to be retrieved when execution is resumed.
This is only useful when interrupting the tool to prompt the user.

enums.label: HookExecutionMode
description:
Determines when the hook is executed relative to the main agent execution flow.

- blocking: executed before proceeding; errors abort the main agent execution.
- nonBlocking: executed in the background; errors are logged and do not abort.

enums.label: HookLifecycle
description:
Lifecycle events that can trigger hooks.

Note: this is intentionally scoped to server-side agent execution lifecycle.

misc.label: AgentEventEmitterFn
description: Event handler function to listen to run events during execution of tools, agents or other agentBuilder primitives.

misc.label: AgentExecution
description:
The agent execution document persisted in the agent-executions index.
Discriminated union on `executionMode`.

misc.label: AgentExecutionParams
description: Union of all execution parameter types.

misc.label: BuiltInPluginDefinition
description:
Represents a built-in plugin definition, as registered by solution teams
using the plugins setup contract.

Built-in plugins are read-only and self-contained: they embed their skill
definitions directly, so registration is a single call. Under the hood,
the plugin service registers those skills into the skill registry with
their `plugin_id` set, mirroring how installable plugins work.

misc.label: chatSystemIndexPrefix
description:
Prefix of the chat system indices.

The Kibana system user has the same permission on those indices than it has on Kibana system indices.

misc.label: ToolReturnSummarizerFn
description:
Function to summarize a tool return for conversation history.
Used to reduce context size by replacing large tool results with compact summaries.

This function receives all results from a single tool call, allowing it to
aggregate and summarize multiple results together (e.g., converting 10 search
results into a single summary like "search returned 10 docs, ids are: ...").
returnComment: The summarized results, or undefined if no summarization should be applied

children.label: toolReturn
description: - All results from a single tool call
